LAW AND MOTION, 302, DEFENDANTS PATH-WISE CORPORATION, ROBERT MULLENDORE AND PRAKASH MUTTINENI'S RE-NOTICE OF MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE AN AMENDED ANSWER. NO APPEARANCE ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING - OFF CALENDAR. OPPOSITION DEMONSTRATES THAT DEFENDANT IS A CORPORATION WHOSE RIGHTS HAVE BEEN FORFEITED. ACCORDINGLY, DEFENDANT CANNOT DEFEND THIS ACTION. THE COURT WOULD CONTINUE THIS MATTER TO ALLOW DEFENDANT TO OBTAIN A CERTIFICATE OF REVIVOR AND/OR APPLY TO REGAIN ITS RIGHTS. SEE COLOR-VUE, INC. VS.ABRAMS (1996) 44 CAL.APP. 4TH 1599. HOWEVER, GIVEN THE 2/22/10 TRIAL DATE IT CANNOT DO SO. DEFENDANT TO APPEAR AT 2/22/10 TRIAL CALL AND EXPLAIN EFFORTS TO REVIVE. JUDGE: A. JAMES ROBERTSON II, REPORTER: JOYCE LONG, CSR #8620

(Amended) Notice And Re Notice Of Motion For Leave To File An Amended Answer
Off Calendar Feb-18-2010 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS PATH-WISE CORPORATION, ROBERT MULLENDORE AND PRAKASH MUTTINENI'S RE-NOTICE OF MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE AN AMENDED ANSWER.
RULING: No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling - Off Calendar. Opposition demonstrates that defendant is a corporation whose rights have been forfeited. Accordingly, Defendant cannot defend this action. The court would continue this matter to allow defendant to obtain a certificate of revivor and/or apply to regain its rights. See Color-Vue, Inc. vs.Abrams (1996) 44 Cal.App. 4th 1599. However, given the 2/22/10 trial date it cannot do so. Defendant to appear at 2/22/10 trial call and explain efforts to revive. (302/AJR) JUDGE: A. JAMES ROBERTSON II, REPORTER: JOYCE LONG, CSR# 8620
